subject: Re: additional pics
body:
ok


----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, June 17, 2008 12:24:53 PM
Subject: FW: additional pics


 
 
From:lance calloway [mailto:lancecalloway@gmail.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, June 17, 2008 3:19 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: additional pics
 
Lesley,
 
Here are some additional pics of the heli pad front wall.  It is sloped at that angle for the reason of being sturdy enough not to fall when a substantial rain comes.  Tom actually laid the area out its on a 1x1 grade meaning for every 1 foot back you go 1 foot up.  This is a 4 foot total grade.  Basically anything more steep than this it will have greater chance of falling down or apart through time.
 
As far as the top goes we can either go up to the edge and hydroseed or lay a rock edge with mud and it will be a non-defined edge sort of like the Owners Drive.  (I can have the guys work on a mock sample of this 1st thing in the morning)  
 
Thanks
 
Lance

-- 
Lance Calloway
Little St. James
6100 Red Hook Quarters Suite B3
St. Thomas, USVI 00802
Lancecalloway@gmail.com
Cell 340-513-9855
Fax 340-775-8108

Lesley,

 

Here are some additional pics of the heli pad front wall.  It is sloped at that angle for the reason of being sturdy enough not to fall when a substantial rain comes.  Tom actually laid the area out its on a 1x1 grade meaning for every 1 foot back you go 1 foot up.  This is a 4 foot total grade.  Basically anything more steep than this it will have greater chance of falling down or apart through time.

 

As far as the top goes we can either go up to the edge and hydroseed or lay a rock edge with mud and it will be a non-defined edge sort of like the Owners Drive.  (I can have the guys work on a mock sample of this 1st thing in the morning)
